operations.name: Decide whether to use CrewAI
operations.trigger: User describes a task involving team/collaboration/roles or explicitly mentions CrewAI
operations.action: Run Phase 0 decision tree: (1) multi-role check; (2) control-flow check. Output: use-CrewAI-Sequential | use-CrewAI-Flow | switch-framework

operations.name: Design agent role/goal/backstory triplet
operations.action: For each agent: write seniority-bearing role title; write goal with template vars + verifiable criteria; write backstory (2-4 sentences) with experience, biases, taboos. Set allow_delegation=False, max_iter=8, verbose=True by default
operations.output: agents.yaml or list of Agent() calls

operations.name: Write Tasks with explicit contracts
operations.action: description = WHAT (not how); expected_output = verifiable assertion; context=[prior_task_refs] explicit; optional output_pydantic for structured outputs
operations.output: tasks.yaml or list of Task() calls

operations.name: Select Process
operations.action: Default Process.sequential. Use Process.hierarchical ONLY with custom manager_agent (never bare manager_llm). If routing logic fits in 5 lines of Python, use Flow instead

operations.name: Decide on Memory
operations.action: Intra-kickoff context => use task.context (memory OFF). Cross-kickoff persistence => memory=True. Production HF => Mem0 backend. Debug period => always OFF

operations.name: Install observability and guardrails before production
operations.action: Attach mlflow.crewai.autolog() OR Maxim/Langfuse/Datadog. Set max_rpm, per-agent max_iter, outer kickoff timeout. Pass step_callback for action logs

operations.name: Upgrade Crew to Flow when needed
operations.trigger: Conditional routing required OR multiple Crews must chain OR hierarchical proven uncontrollable
operations.action: Subclass Flow[StateModel]. Use @start() entry; @listen(prior) for branches. Each step can be plain Python, single LLM call, or crew.kickoff()
operations.output: Flow class with deterministic spine + autonomous Crews per node

operations.name: Scope tools per-role
operations.trigger: Multiple tools available across multiple agents
operations.action: Define tools once (BaseTool/@tool). Bind each agent only to role-relevant tools. Avoid blanket tool sharing

operations.name: Default allow_delegation=False on all worker agents
operations.trigger: Agent creation, especially in hierarchical mode
operations.action: Workers always allow_delegation=False; only manager_agent (if any) gets delegation. Ensure at least one agent in chain has it disabled to break cycles

operations.name: Split agents on persistent failure modes
operations.trigger: Agent fails to converge after 3-5 iterations on real cases
operations.action: Classify failure: style inconsistency => fix backstory; missing checklist items / factual errors / format violations => split into specialist + reviewer with structured rubric
operations.output: Refactored crew with sharper role separation

anti_patterns_summary: AP-1 Agent inflation (>5 agents without strong justification)
AP-3 Default hierarchical manager_llm in production
dilemma_cases_summary: DC-1 Agent looping: fix prompt vs split agent
DC-2 Sequential vs Hierarchical: when is manager overhead worth it
DC-3 Tools: shared all-agents vs role-scoped
DC-5 allow_delegation: on vs off (ping-pong risk)

key_quotes.text: An agent needs agency, otherwise it's just another script.
Role, goal, and backstory are required and shape the agent's behavior.
Single-agent is right for approximately 80% of cases.
Crews enable autonomous collaboration; Flows offer precise, event-driven control.
